ops.text:
PRE-REGISTERED PROTOCOL (before any LLM run). Cap $10; judge=opus.

GOAL: Re-label 200 triage-labeled rows under a counterfactual INTERVENTION framing (arXiv:2510.01545 style) instead of the triage taxonomy question, then build the agreement matrix (triage is_steering x intervention would_change) per category, identify divergent categories, and characterize disagreeing rows (informs E26 loss choice + E28 label cleaning).

UNIVERSE: latest_judge WHERE model='opus' (4692 rows, all prompt_version=6) from snapshot /Users/yasyf/.cc-steer/experiments/_base-20260719/feedback.db (read-only, immutable=1). Restricting to opus isolates the FRAMING effect from any judge-model confound. In pv6 the category deterministically maps to is_steering, so stratifying by category == stratifying by verdict.

SAMPLE: n=200, seed=25, per-category without replacement, sorted by dedup_key for reproducibility. Non-proportional allocation (over-samples divergence-candidate noise cats for characterization power): direction 40, wrong_approach 25, operational_directive 25, new_task 22, status_update 18, incorrect_change 15, question 15, unwanted_action 12, style_violation 12, premature 10, other 6. Each <= available.

CONTEXT RENDER: forced SUMMARY fidelity for all rows via replace(ContextWindow.from_json(context_json), fidelity='summary').render_preview(budget=Budget()) — the exact production summary path (triage.render_context hydrate-is-None branch). Chosen because (a) 170/200 triage baselines were already judged at summary fidelity so it is the matched context for 85% of rows, (b) it is stable/reproducible from persisted previews (no transient transcript liveness), (c) removes fidelity as a confound, (d) caps cost (~368k input tokens, ~$3). The 30 rows whose triage verdict was at 'full' fidelity are flagged; agreement reported with and without them (sensitivity).

INTERVENTION PROMPT (prompt_version=int-v1): reframes from "is this steering?" to a counterfactual: "if the assistant had seen this exact message BEFORE it acted, would its very next action/output DIFFER from its default continuation, and how?" Outputs would_change in {yes,no}; change_kind in {corrective, resolve_choice, new_work, convention, none}; how (1 sentence); confidence; rationale. Deliberately DROPS triage's 'must fault work or resolve a choice the ASSISTANT raised' constraint — the framings are EXPECTED to diverge on operational_directive/new_task (behaviorally active but triage-noise). Full prompt text in scripts/intervention.py.

MAPPING: intervention label = would_change (yes->steering-equivalent, no->noise-equivalent). Agreement = (triage.is_steering == (would_change=='yes')).

SUCCESS CRITERION: produce (1) agreement_overall (raw sample AND population-reweighted by category share) with bootstrap 95% CI; (2) per_category agreement + n + CI; (3) divergent_categories ranked by disagreement rate; (4) qualitative characterization of disagreeing rows keyed by change_kind. PENDING-E15 discipline: point estimates + bootstrap CIs only, NO promote/kill verdict. Success = matrix built + divergence characterized with raw artifacts retained; not a threshold on agreement.

ARTIFACTS: out/sample.jsonl (200 sampled keys), out/labels.jsonl (per-row intervention labels), out/agreement.json (matrix + stats), out/divergent.jsonl (disagreeing rows w/ text+how). Raw kept for verify lane re-derivation.
